meaning: n.耙(地),使精神痛苦vt.耙掘,伤害,使苦恼vi.被耙松
examples: Churchill went to Harrow Public School.
At the age of twelve, his father sent him to Harrow Public School, which was famous for training English gentlemen.
So did Winston Churchill, who almost failed out of Harrow, an elite British school.
The waste will come from Brent, Ealing, Harrow, Hillingdon, Hounslow and Richmond upon Thames.
As a boy, the former British PM won a poetry prize at Harrow School.
